# Code RED source/decompiled export
# Source: docs/CodeRED_Living_World_Station_Prep.md
# SHA1: 5204131bd0d4ca28d00b274b25429980a802b7cc

# Code RED Living World Station Prep

Purpose: prepare the next faction-war and living-world pass without losing older progress.

## Direction

Code RED should keep doing what worked best:

- raw-file patching first
- copied archive builders
- patch manifests and reopen validation
- placement, tune, refgroup, population, and event edits
- in-game plugin work only when raw files cannot express the behavior

The goal is to build one larger living-world system from the proven faction-war work instead of bouncing between unrelated experiments.

## Known strong baseline

Use the Pass 15 / Pass 15.30 validated lineage as the first reconstruction map. The validated manifest says it was a Blackwater Chaos + Faction War full rebase built from:

- Pass 12 wilderness living-world base
- Pass 13 empty-world placement lane
- Pass 14 Blackwater plus lasso tune lane
- strings research as reference only

Important preserved checks from that manifest included:

- Pass 12 wilderness living-world set
- Pass 12 empty-country trouble set
- Pass 13 empty-world activity set
- Pass 14 Blackwater chaos set
- rowdy gangs
- lone lawman
- duels
- law high alert
- pathfinding deny loosened
- playerally gang relations preserved
- content companion AI pulse preserved
- tune companion AI pulse preserved
- wilderness population preserved
- default traffic routes preserved
- ambient actor cap 130 preserved
- lasso, base_lasso, and sheriff tune changes applied

## No-regression rule

Before adding the user performance/world mod or any new living-world changes:

1. Inventory every older faction-war pass still in the repo.
2. Extract each pass manifest/report if present.
3. Build a feature matrix by pass.
4. Mark features as proven, uncertain, or missing from the current baseline.
5. Do not overwrite the Pass 12/13/14/15 lineage until the matrix is complete.
6. Keep copied archive workflows only.
7. Add the new performance mod as a separate layer, then compare it against the validated faction-war feature matrix.

## First easy-pass objective

Do not start with hard live-spawn behavior.

Start by confirming raw-file edits still work:

- placementglobals.xml event allow-set changes
- tune setting changes
- level_pop and population pressure changes
- refgroup and camp persistence changes
- traffic and roadside activity changes
- simple law, gang, and event pressure changes

## Reload test order

When testing raw-file edits, determine the fastest reload path in this order:

1. load save
2. reload save
3. exit to title/menu and reload
4. restart game
5. only then investigate cache behavior

Do not clear caches blindly.

## Next likely pass name

Code RED Living World Pass 16 - No-Regression Raw-File Rebuild

Primary job: restore the strongest faction-war/living-world baseline, add the user's performance mod as a separate layer, and prove which easy raw-file edits still apply before attempting harder live-control behaviors.
